name: Hearty Burgundy Meatballs
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Fresh bread crumbs, About 2 slices bread
3 medium Carrots, peeled and sliced, About 1 cup
2 cups Quartered medium-size fresh Mushrooms, about 8 oz.
8 oz Pearl onions, peeled and halved, about 1 1/2 cups
1/2 cup Red burgundy or other dry red wine
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine beef, egg, bread crumbs, salt and pepper. Using hands or a wooden spoon, blend well.
2. Shape the mixture into 1 1/4 inch balls.
3. Heat oil in a 12-inch skillet over medium-high heat. Add meatballs and cook about 12 minutes, turning frequently until well browned on all sides.
4. Remove meatballs to a plate using a slotted spoon.
5. In the drippings in the skillet, add carrots, mushrooms, and onions; cook still over medium-high heat, about 5 minutes, stirring frequently until crisp-tender.
6. Stir in flour to coat vegetables well; return meatballs to the skillet along with wine, bouillon, 2 tbsp parsley, and 1 cup water.
7. Increase heat to high and bring to a boil. Reduce heat to low and simmer, covered, for 10 minutes until meatballs are cooked through.
8. Spoon meatballs and vegetables into a serving bowl; sprinkle with the remaining tablespoon parsley.
9. Serve accompanied by French bread. Makes 4 servings.
